• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

WINN Comparison Notebook

This notebook compares WINN against commonly used metabolomics correction methods, including QC‑RLSC, QC‑RFSC, QC‑LOESS, SERRF, and ComBat. It also includes practical baselines (raw and PQN-only) and a widely used batch‑correction alternative (limma::removeBatchEffect).

All metrics are computed on simulated LC‑MS data with known drift, batch effects, and sample‑wise dilution. Biological groups are randomized within each batch and QC samples appear every 8th measurement.

1. Dependencies

This notebook uses a few optional packages. If some are missing, those methods will be skipped.

req <- function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    message("Package not installed: ", pkg)
    return(FALSE)
  }
  TRUE
}

has_winn <- req("winn")
has_sva <- req("sva")
has_limma <- req("limma")
has_statTarget <- req("statTarget")
has_serrf <- req("SERRF")
#> Package not installed: SERRF
serrf_pkg <- NULL
if (!has_serrf) {
  has_serrf <- req("serrf")
  if (has_serrf) serrf_pkg <- "serrf"
} else {
  serrf_pkg <- "SERRF"
}
#> Package not installed: serrf
has_qcrlscR <- req("qcrlscR")

2. Simulate LC‑MS data with drift + batch effects

set.seed(42)

n_met <- 50
n_samples <- 201
n_batches <- 4
samples_per_batch <- floor(n_samples / n_batches)

batch <- rep(seq_len(n_batches), each = samples_per_batch, length.out = n_samples)
run_order <- seq_len(n_samples)

qc_idx <- unique(c(seq(1, n_samples, by = 8), n_samples))
non_qc_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(n_samples), qc_idx)

# Randomize biological groups along measurement order (QC excluded),
# with equal A/B counts within each batch
group <- rep(NA_character_, n_samples)
group[qc_idx] <- "QC"
for (b in seq_len(n_batches)) {
  idx_b <- which(batch == b)
  non_qc_b <- intersect(idx_b, non_qc_idx)
  n_b <- length(non_qc_b)
  labels <- rep(c("A", "B"), length.out = n_b)
  group[non_qc_b] <- sample(labels)
}

# True log-scale signal (no drift; centered around metabolite-specific mean)
base_mean <- rnorm(n_met, mean = 8, sd = 0.5)
baseline_log <- base_mean + matrix(rnorm(n_met * n_samples, mean = 0, sd = 0.1), nrow = n_met)

# Binary group effect (10–20% of metabolites)
effect_prop <- runif(1, 0.10, 0.20)
effect_count <- max(1, round(n_met * effect_prop))
effect_metabolites <- sort(sample(seq_len(n_met), effect_count))
true_effect <- rep(0, n_met)
true_effect[effect_metabolites] <- rnorm(effect_count, mean = 0.8, sd = 0.1)

true_log <- baseline_log
true_log[effect_metabolites, group == "B"] <- true_log[effect_metabolites, group == "B"] +
  true_effect[effect_metabolites]

# Continuous covariate (5–10% of metabolites), independent of run order
cont_var <- runif(n_samples, min = 0, max = 1)
cont_var[qc_idx] <- 0
cont_prop <- runif(1, 0.05, 0.10)
cont_count <- max(1, round(n_met * cont_prop))
cont_metabolites <- setdiff(seq_len(n_met), effect_metabolites)
cont_metabolites <- sort(sample(cont_metabolites, cont_count))
cont_effect <- rep(0, n_met)
# Weaker effect size (0.6-0.9 instead of 1.2) so drift/batch noise matters more
cont_effect[cont_metabolites] <- rnorm(cont_count, mean = 0.75, sd = 0.08)
true_log[cont_metabolites, ] <- true_log[cont_metabolites, ] +
  cont_effect[cont_metabolites] %*% t(cont_var)

# QC samples: pooled reference
pooled_log <- rowMeans(true_log[, group != "QC", drop = FALSE])
true_log[, qc_idx] <- pooled_log

# Sample-wise dilution (multiplicative)
dilution_factor <- exp(rnorm(n_samples, mean = 0, sd = 0.08))

# Drift per batch (warm-up -> plateau -> decline with non-linear curvature)
smoothstep <- function(x) {
  x <- pmin(pmax(x, 0), 1)
  x * x * (3 - 2 * x)
}

make_batch_drift <- function(n, shape = c("linearish", "nonlinear")) {
  shape <- match.arg(shape)
  n1 <- floor(n * 0.30)
  n2 <- floor(n * 0.45)
  n3 <- n - n1 - n2

  up <- smoothstep(seq(0, 1, length.out = n1))
  flat <- rep(1, n2)
  down <- 1 - smoothstep(seq(0, 1, length.out = n3))
  base <- c(up, flat, down)

  curve_strength <- ifelse(shape == "nonlinear", 0.15, 0.06)
  curve <- curve_strength * (seq_len(n) / n - 0.5)^2
  rw <- cumsum(rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = ifelse(shape == "nonlinear", 0.012, 0.008)))
  base <- base + curve + rw

  base <- base - min(base)
  base <- base / max(base)
  base
}

drift_log <- matrix(0, nrow = n_met, ncol = n_samples)
# 20-25% with strong drift, 20-25% with minimal drift, rest moderate
drift_tier <- runif(n_met)
strong_mask <- drift_tier < 0.25
low_mask <- drift_tier >= 0.75
drift_severity <- numeric(n_met)
drift_severity[strong_mask] <- runif(sum(strong_mask), 0.20, 0.38)
drift_severity[low_mask] <- runif(sum(low_mask), 0.00, 0.03)
drift_severity[!(strong_mask | low_mask)] <- runif(sum(!(strong_mask | low_mask)), 0.10, 0.18)
amp <- rnorm(n_met, mean = drift_severity, sd = pmax(drift_severity * 0.2, 0.01))
for (b in seq_len(n_batches)) {
  idx <- which(batch == b)
  shape <- ifelse(b %% 2 == 0, "nonlinear", "linearish")
  trend_b <- make_batch_drift(length(idx), shape = shape)
  drift_log[, idx] <- amp %*% t(trend_b)
}

# Batch effects (per-metabolite, variable severity)
batch_severity <- numeric(n_met)
batch_severity[strong_mask] <- runif(sum(strong_mask), 0.40, 0.80)
batch_severity[low_mask] <- runif(sum(low_mask), 0.00, 0.04)
batch_severity[!(strong_mask | low_mask)] <- runif(sum(!(strong_mask | low_mask)), 0.06, 0.20)
batch_shift_mat <- sapply(seq_len(n_batches), function(b) rnorm(n_met, mean = 0, sd = batch_severity))
batch_scale_mat <- sapply(seq_len(n_batches), function(b) exp(rnorm(n_met, mean = 0, sd = batch_severity / 2)))

batch_log <- matrix(0, nrow = n_met, ncol = n_samples)
for (b in seq_len(n_batches)) {
  idx <- which(batch == b)
  batch_log[, idx] <- batch_shift_mat[, b] + log(batch_scale_mat[, b])
}

# Homoscedastic noise (constant variance across intensities)
noise_log <- matrix(rnorm(n_met * n_samples, mean = 0, sd = 0.12), nrow = n_met)

# Observed data in intensity space
observed_log <- true_log +
  matrix(log(dilution_factor), nrow = n_met, ncol = n_samples, byrow = TRUE) +
  drift_log +
  batch_log +
  noise_log

true_intensity <- pmax(expm1(true_log), 0)
observed_intensity <- pmax(expm1(observed_log), 0)

# Add sample names (used by some methods)
colnames(observed_intensity) <- paste0("S", seq_len(n_samples))
colnames(true_intensity) <- colnames(observed_intensity)

3. Run correction methods

if (!has_winn) stop("winn package is required for this notebook")

library(winn)

# WINN
winn_without_qcs <- winn(
  observed_intensity,
  batch = batch,
  run_order = run_order,
  median_adjustment = "shrink",
  remove_batch_effects = "anova",
  scale_by_batch = FALSE
)
#> Starting Winn correction...
#> Adjusting outliers using MAD...
#> Correcting drift using autocorrelation test (Ljung-Box) with FDR threshold: 0.05...
#> Warning in smooth.construct.ps.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots): there is
#> *no* information about some basis coefficients
#> Correcting batch effects using ANOVA-based residualization with FDR threshold: 0.05...
#> Performing median adjustment using method: shrink
#> Winn correction completed.

winn_qc_auto <- winn(
  observed_intensity,
  batch = batch,
  run_order = run_order,
  control_samples = qc_idx,
  parameters = "auto",
  median_adjustment = "shrink",
  remove_batch_effects = "anova",
  scale_by_batch = FALSE
)
#> Starting Winn correction...
#> Adjusting outliers using MAD...
#> Auto-detecting optimal parameters using control samples...
#> Warning in smooth.construct.ps.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots): there is
#> *no* information about some basis coefficients
#> Warning in smooth.construct.ps.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots): there is
#> *no* information about some basis coefficients
#> Parameter optimization progress: 9.7%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 19.4%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 29.2%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 38.9%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 48.6%
#> Warning in smooth.construct.ps.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots): there is
#> *no* information about some basis coefficients
#> Parameter optimization progress: 58.3%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 68.1%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 77.8%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 87.5%
#> Parameter optimization progress: 97.2%
#> Optimal parameters selected based on control samples:
#>   Batch detection: provided
#>   Autocorrelation test: DW (FDR: 0.1)
#>   Spline method: standard
#>   Batch correction FDR: 0.1
#>   Normalization: normalize
#>   Scale by batch: FALSE
#>   Quality metrics - CV: 0.1253, Correlation: 0.9411
#> Winn correction completed.

# PQN only (dilution normalization)
pqn_only <- normalize_by_dilution_factor(
  observed_intensity,
  processing = "normalize",
  control_samples = qc_idx
)

# ComBat (sva)
combat_corrected <- if (has_sva) {
  combat_batch_correction(observed_intensity, batch = batch)
} else {
  NULL
}
#> Found4batches
#> Adjusting for0covariate(s) or covariate level(s)
#> Standardizing Data across genes
#> Fitting L/S model and finding priors
#> Finding parametric adjustments
#> Adjusting the Data

# limma::removeBatchEffect (log1p space)
limma_corrected <- if (has_limma) {
  x_log <- log1p(observed_intensity)
  y_log <- limma::removeBatchEffect(x_log, batch = batch)
  pmax(expm1(y_log), 0)
} else {
  NULL
}

# QC-RLSC (qcrlscR)
qc_rlsc_corrected <- if (has_qcrlscR) {
  dat <- t(observed_intensity)  # samples x metabolites
  y <- ifelse(group == "QC", "QC", "Sample")
  res <- qcrlscR::qc.rlsc(
    dat,
    y = y,
    sample = run_order,
    method = "divide"
  )
  t(as.matrix(res))
} else {
  NULL
}
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in sqrt(sum.squares/one.delta): NaNs produced
#> Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
#> : span too small.  fewer data values than degrees of freedom.
#> Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
#> : pseudoinverse used at 1
#> Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
#> : neighborhood radius 16
#> Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
#> : reciprocal condition number 0
#> Warning in simpleLoess(y, x, w, span, degree = degree, parametric = parametric,
#> : There are other near singularities as well. 64
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in sqrt(sum.squares/one.delta): NaNs produced
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value
#> Warning in optimize(f, spans): NA/Inf replaced by maximum positive value

# QC-RFSC / QC-RLSC (statTarget::shiftCor)
run_shiftcor <- function(method) {
  if (!has_statTarget) return(NULL)

  # prepare metadata
  sample_names <- colnames(observed_intensity)
  meta <- data.frame(
    sample = sample_names,
    batch = batch,
    class = ifelse(group == "QC", NA, "Sample"),
    order = run_order,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Ensure ordering by run order and QC at first/last (statTarget requirement)
  meta <- meta[order(meta$order), ]
  if (!is.na(meta$class[1]) || !is.na(meta$class[nrow(meta)])) {
    message("statTarget requires QC as first and last sample (by run order). Skipping ", method, ".")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # prepare expression file (features x samples with header)
  samFile_df <- data.frame(
    name = paste0("M", seq_len(n_met)),
    observed_intensity,
    check.names = FALSE
  )
  samFile_df <- samFile_df[, c("name", meta$sample), drop = FALSE]
  meta$sample[which(is.na(meta$class))] <- paste0(meta$sample[which(is.na(meta$class))], "_QC")
  colnames(samFile_df)[2:ncol(samFile_df)] <- meta$sample

  tmp <- tempfile("shiftcor_")
  dir.create(tmp)
  old <- getwd()
  setwd(tmp)
  on.exit(setwd(old), add = TRUE)

  samPeno_file <- file.path(tmp, "samPeno.csv")
  samFile_file <- file.path(tmp, "samFile.csv")

  write.csv(meta, samPeno_file, row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
  write.csv(samFile_df, samFile_file, row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

  statTarget::shiftCor(
    samPeno = samPeno_file,
    samFile = samFile_file,
    Frule = 0.8,
    MLmethod = method
  )

  out_file <- file.path("statTarget", "shiftCor", "After_shiftCor", "shift_all_cor.csv")
  if (!file.exists(out_file)) return(NULL)

  df <- read.csv(out_file, check.names = FALSE)
  # First row and column are sample and class
  mat <- apply(as.matrix(df[-1, -1, drop = FALSE]), 2, as.numeric)
  rownames(mat) <- df$sample[-1]
  mat[, meta$sample, drop = FALSE]
}

qc_rfsc_corrected <- run_shiftcor("QCRFSC")
#>

#> Warning in dir.create(dirout.w):
#> '/private/var/folders/6b/6d5krtz91kv31fyxg_zb3m4m0000gq/T/RtmpDvNrzB/shiftcor_129a86e446fad/statTarget/shiftCor/RSDresult'
#> already exists
#>         CV<5% CV<10% CV<15% CV<20% CV<25% CV<30% CV<35% CV<40% CV<45% CV<50%
#> Batch_1     4     44     92     96     98    100    100    100    100    100
#> Batch_2     8     56     90     96     96     98    100    100    100    100
#> Batch_3     6     66     92     96     96    100    100    100    100    100
#> Batch_4     2     40     88     98    100    100    100    100    100    100
#> Total       0     60     90     98    100    100    100    100    100    100
#>         CV<55% CV<60% CV<65% CV<70% CV<75% CV<80% CV<85% CV<90% CV<95% CV<100%
#> Batch_1    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100     100
#> Batch_2    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100     100
#> Batch_3    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100     100
#> Batch_4    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100     100
#> Total      100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100    100     100
#> 
#> * Step 5:  Removal of the features (CV% > 30%) Fri Feb  6 11:34:41 2026 
#> 
#>  No. of removed features: 0
#>  Feature name:  
#> 
#>  Output Link: /private/var/folders/6b/6d5krtz91kv31fyxg_zb3m4m0000gq/T/RtmpDvNrzB/shiftcor_129a86e446fad 
#> 
#>  Correction Finished! Time:  Fri Feb  6 11:34:41 2026 
#> 
#>  #################################### 
#>  # Software Version: statTarget 2.0 + # 
#>  # Email: hemi.luan@gmail.com # 
#>  ####################################
#qc_rlsc_statTarget <- run_shiftcor("QCRLSC")
qc_rlsc_statTarget <- NULL

# QC-LOESS (base R; widely used QC-based drift correction)
qc_loess_corrected <- {
  if (length(qc_idx) < 5) {
    NULL
  } else {
    x_log <- log1p(observed_intensity)
    corrected <- x_log
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(x_log))) {
      y_qc <- x_log[i, qc_idx]
      fit <- tryCatch(
        loess(y_qc ~ run_order[qc_idx], span = 0.75, degree = 1),
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
      if (is.null(fit)) next
      pred <- predict(fit, newdata = run_order)
      ref <- mean(pred[qc_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
      corrected[i, ] <- x_log[i, ] - (pred - ref)
    }
    pmax(expm1(corrected), 0)
  }
}

# SERRF (optional, if package is available)
serrf_corrected <- if (has_serrf) {
  tryCatch({
    ns <- asNamespace(serrf_pkg)
    exports <- getNamespaceExports(serrf_pkg)
    fn_name <- if ("SERRF" %in% exports) {
      "SERRF"
    } else if ("serrf" %in% exports) {
      "serrf"
    } else {
      NULL
    }

    if (is.null(fn_name)) return(NULL)

    fn <- get(fn_name, ns)
    dat <- t(observed_intensity)  # samples x metabolites
    sample_type <- ifelse(group == "QC", "QC", "Sample")

    res <- tryCatch({
      fn(dat, sampleType = sample_type, order = run_order)
    }, error = function(e) {
      fn(dat, sampleType = sample_type)
    })

    t(as.matrix(res))
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("SERRF failed: ", conditionMessage(e))
    NULL
  })
} else {
  NULL
}

4. Metrics

We report:

  • Mean per‑metabolite correlation with the true signal (log1p scale)
  • Mean QC CV (lower is better)
  • Biological effect preservation: for metabolites with true effects, we compute 1 / (1 + |estimated_effect − true_effect|) (higher is better)
  • Continuous covariate preservation: same 1 / (1 + |error|) metric on per‑metabolite slopes
calc_metric_vectors <- function(x, truth, group, qc_idx, effect_metabolites, cont_var, cont_metabolites) {
  # log-space
  x_log <- log1p(x)
  truth_log <- log1p(truth)

  # 1) per-metabolite correlation between corrected and true
  cor_vec <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(x_log)), function(i) {
    cor(x_log[i, ], truth_log[i, ], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  })

  # 2) QC CV per metabolite
  qc_log <- x_log[, qc_idx, drop = FALSE]
  qc_cv_vec <- apply(qc_log, 1, function(v) {
    mu <- mean(v, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (is.na(mu) || mu == 0) return(NA_real_)
    sd(v, na.rm = TRUE) / abs(mu)
  })

  # 3) Effect-size recovery for binary group effects
  est_effect <- rowMeans(x_log[, group == "B", drop = FALSE]) -
    rowMeans(x_log[, group == "A", drop = FALSE])
  true_effect <- rowMeans(truth_log[, group == "B", drop = FALSE]) -
    rowMeans(truth_log[, group == "A", drop = FALSE])

  est_eff_sub <- est_effect[effect_metabolites]
  true_eff_sub <- true_effect[effect_metabolites]
  effect_error <- est_eff_sub - true_eff_sub
  effect_abs_error <- abs(effect_error)

  effect_preserve_vec <- 1 / (1 + effect_abs_error)

  # 4) Continuous covariate recovery: estimate slope per metabolite and compare to truth
  non_qc <- group != "QC"
  fit_slope <- function(y, xcov) {
    if (all(is.na(y)) || length(unique(xcov)) < 2) return(NA_real_)
    m <- tryCatch(coef(lm(y ~ xcov)), error = function(e) c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
    as.numeric(m[2])
  }

  est_slopes <- sapply(cont_metabolites, function(i) fit_slope(x_log[i, non_qc], cont_var[non_qc]))
  true_slopes <- sapply(cont_metabolites, function(i) fit_slope(truth_log[i, non_qc], cont_var[non_qc]))

  cont_slope_error <- est_slopes - true_slopes
  cont_preserve_vec <- 1 / (1 + abs(cont_slope_error))

  list(
    cor_vec = cor_vec,
    qc_cv_vec = qc_cv_vec,
    # effect-size vectors and summary scalars
    effect_preserve_vec = effect_preserve_vec,
    # continuous slopes
    cont_preserve_vec = cont_preserve_vec
  )
}

summarize_metric <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  c(mean = mean(x), se = sd(x) / sqrt(length(x)))
}

# Identify metabolites with true effects
met_names <- paste0("M", seq_len(n_met))
rownames(true_intensity) <- met_names
rownames(observed_intensity) <- met_names
rownames(winn_without_qcs) <- met_names
rownames(winn_qc_auto) <- met_names
rownames(pqn_only) <- met_names
if (!is.null(combat_corrected)) rownames(combat_corrected) <- met_names
if (!is.null(limma_corrected)) rownames(limma_corrected) <- met_names
if (!is.null(qc_rlsc_corrected)) rownames(qc_rlsc_corrected) <- met_names
if (!is.null(qc_rfsc_corrected)) rownames(qc_rfsc_corrected) <- met_names
if (!is.null(qc_rlsc_statTarget)) rownames(qc_rlsc_statTarget) <- met_names
if (!is.null(qc_loess_corrected)) rownames(qc_loess_corrected) <- met_names
if (!is.null(serrf_corrected)) rownames(serrf_corrected) <- met_names

sample_names <- colnames(observed_intensity)
colnames(true_intensity) <- sample_names
colnames(winn_without_qcs) <- sample_names
colnames(winn_qc_auto) <- sample_names
colnames(pqn_only) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(combat_corrected)) colnames(combat_corrected) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(limma_corrected)) colnames(limma_corrected) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(qc_rlsc_corrected)) colnames(qc_rlsc_corrected) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(qc_rfsc_corrected)) colnames(qc_rfsc_corrected) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(qc_rlsc_statTarget)) colnames(qc_rlsc_statTarget) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(qc_loess_corrected)) colnames(qc_loess_corrected) <- sample_names
if (!is.null(serrf_corrected)) colnames(serrf_corrected) <- sample_names

# List of methods to compare
methods <- list(
  Raw = observed_intensity,
  PQN_only = pqn_only,
  WINN_without_QCs = winn_without_qcs,
  WINN_QC_auto = winn_qc_auto,
  ComBat = combat_corrected,
  limma_removeBatchEffect = limma_corrected,
  QC_LOESS = qc_loess_corrected,
  QC_RLSC = qc_rlsc_corrected,
  QC_RFSC = qc_rfsc_corrected,
  QC_RLSC_statTarget = qc_rlsc_statTarget,
  SERRF = serrf_corrected
)

# Remove missing methods
methods <- methods[!sapply(methods, is.null)]

# Compute metrics
metrics_list <- lapply(methods, calc_metric_vectors,
                       truth = true_intensity,
                       group = group,
                       qc_idx = qc_idx,
                       effect_metabolites = effect_metabolites,
                       cont_var = cont_var,
                       cont_metabolites = cont_metabolites)

# Summarize
summary_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(metrics_list), function(name) {
  m <- metrics_list[[name]]
  # vector metrics use summarize_metric; scalar metrics are reported with NA se
  vec_mean_cor <- summarize_metric(m$cor_vec)["mean"]
  vec_se_cor <- summarize_metric(m$cor_vec)["se"]

  vec_mean_qc <- summarize_metric(m$qc_cv_vec)["mean"]
  vec_se_qc <- summarize_metric(m$qc_cv_vec)["se"]

  vec_mean_eff_pres <- summarize_metric(m$effect_preserve_vec)["mean"]
  vec_se_eff_pres <- summarize_metric(m$effect_preserve_vec)["se"]

  vec_mean_cont_pres <- summarize_metric(m$cont_preserve_vec)["mean"]
  vec_se_cont_pres <- summarize_metric(m$cont_preserve_vec)["se"]

  data.frame(
    method = name,
    metric = c(
      "Mean correlation",
      "QC CV",
      "Effect preservation",
      "Continuous preservation"
    ),
    mean = c(
      vec_mean_cor,
      vec_mean_qc,
      vec_mean_eff_pres,
      vec_mean_cont_pres
    ),
    se = c(
      vec_se_cor,
      vec_se_qc,
      vec_se_eff_pres,
      vec_se_cont_pres
    )
  )
}))

summary_rows
summary_rows[,3:4] <- round(summary_rows[,3:4], 4)
if (!requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE)) stop("knitr is required for the results table")
knitr::kable(
  summary_rows,
  caption = "Summary metrics (mean ± SE across metabolites)."
)
Summary metrics (mean ± SE across metabolites).

5. Bar plots with standard error

if (!requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) stop("ggplot2 is required for plotting")
library(ggplot2)
#> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3

# Add continuous covariate metrics to plot table
plot_rows <- summary_rows
plot_rows$method <- factor(plot_rows$method, levels = unique(plot_rows$method))

palette <- c(
  "Raw" = "#5E5E5E",
  "PQN_only" = "#9E9E9E",
  "WINN_without_QCs" = "#2A9D8F",
  "WINN_QC_auto" = "#1F77B4",
  "ComBat" = "#E76F51",
  "limma_removeBatchEffect" = "#8E6C8A",
  "QC_LOESS" = "#4E79A7",
  "QC_RLSC" = "#59A14F",
  "QC_RFSC" = "#F28E2B",
  "QC_RLSC_statTarget" = "#B07AA1",
  "SERRF" = "#EDC948"
)

ggplot(plot_rows, aes(x = method, y = mean, fill = method)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
                width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette, drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Method Comparison on Simulated LC-MS Data",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean ± SE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

Notes

  • QC‑RFSC and QC‑RLSC are run via statTarget::shiftCor when available.
  • If those packages are not installed, the corresponding methods are skipped.
  • All metrics are computed in a consistent way across methods.
